subject: Project Management
title: Movie Budgets That Ballooned
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Movie Budgets That Ballooned.

hook.text: Cleopatra (1963) was budgeted at $2 million. It cost $44 million and nearly bankrupted 20th Century Fox.

buildup.text: Waterworld, Titanic, and The Hobbit trilogy all massively exceeded their original budgets.

discovery.text: Movie production faces the same forces as any project: scope creep, optimism, and sunk cost traps.

twist.text: Titanic was $100 million over budget — and became the highest-grossing film ever. Success forgives overruns.

climax.text: Hollywood proves the planning fallacy isn't industry-specific — it's fundamentally human.

punchline.text: Even make-believe worlds blow real budgets.

quiz.question: How much did Cleopatra (1963) exceed its original budget?
options: From $2 million to $44 million — a 22x increase
It doubled from $20 million to $40 million
